entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Complete assigned models, focusing on craftsmanship. 2nd-Year Models include more complex envelopes and boxes.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
Grade 3 students who are new to sloyd should take Sloyd: Intro and then continue the courses in sequence.

Prerequisite: Sloyd: Level 1

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Sloyd: Level 2
☐ View the "Sloyd Introduction Video" for an overview of materials and resources used in Sloyd (see Quick Links)

☐ Purchase supplies and organize them so they are easily accessible. 

☐ Keep the page of images for each of the second-year models for reference while working on models (see Appendix). 

☐ Allow time for loading and viewing videos. We recommend teachers preview tutorials and work a model before teaching it.

☐ Before each lesson, gather needed materials as listed at the beginning of each day's lesson plan. 

☐ Introduce students to their Sloyd tools (discuss their care and safe use). Refresh students on how to use a ruler (note where "0" starts) and how to use the edge to draw a straight line.

blocks.text: Lessons will go much more smoothly if you prepare thoroughly. As students gain experience, develop fine motor skills, and practice habits of precision they will need less support.
Watch video tutorials or make the model yourself. Gather supplies and materials needed.
Connect back to previous lesson and skills learned.
Read the instructions one step at a time. Model each step and make sure students complete it with precision before moving to the next step.
When a project is complete, students may repeat it as many times as they wish to give as gifts or create original patterns similar to the project.

lessons.title: 20m Sloyd: Level 2 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Model 1 Conical Flower Holder
⍞ Materials: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades, cardstock, ruler, pencil, scissors, tape, compass, glue, t-square (optional)

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Conical Flower Holder

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Second Year Models "III. Conical Flower Holder" p.42
Stuff this conical flower holder with candy or other small trinkets, and give it as a gift. It is also great to use as a party favor. You can change the color of cardstock to fit your event theme.

Second Year Model 2 Stamp Book
⍞ Materials: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades, cardstock, ruler, pencil, scissors, tape, cutting mat/scrap cardboard, awl, X-Acto knife, wax paper, stapler

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Stamp Book

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Second Year Models "II. Stamp Book" p.26 
Stamp collecting is out, but sticker collecting is in. Use this book to collect stickers instead.

lessons.title: 20m Sloyd: Level 2 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
Second Year Model 5 Thread Winder
⍞ Materials: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades, cardstock, ruler, pencil, scissors, tape, hole punch (optional), string (optional)

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Thread Winder

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Second Year Models "V. Thread Winder" p.29
Thread winders are great for organizing smaller pieces of thread or yarn that are too big to discard but tend to get tangled up if not properly stored.

⍞ Materials: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades, cardstock, ruler, pencil, scissors, tape, awl, bone, glue stick

Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Handkerchief Box

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Second Year Models "VII. Handkerchief Box" p.31
The handkerchief box is great for organizing seeds from your garden. Make several, label the outside, and stack them for storage until next season.

lessons.title: 20m Sloyd: Level 2 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Second Year Model 9 Folding Card Case
⍞ Materials: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades, cardstock, ruler, pencil, scissors, tape, awl, bone, glue stick

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Folding Card Case

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Second Year Models "IX. Folding Card Case" p.33
The folding card case is a great way to gift cash, stickers, and gift cards. You can also write a note of encouragement, tuck it into the case, attach it to a gift, or hand it freely to a loved one.

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Envelope

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Second Year Models "X. Envelope" p.34
Handwritten letters are more precious than ever before to send to a friend or family member. Be sure to glue it well so that it holds up in the mail.

➜ PRACTICE

Second Year Models "XI.-XIV." p.35-38 
Find ways to use these items in your life today.
